column5: Use Cases of Aura AI & Sparse AI in example industries
column6:
Aura AI:

From Static Content to Guided Decision Experiences - Retail — Retail websites are rich in content but poor at guidance. Customers struggle to find the right product, understand differences, and make confident purchase decisions—leading to high bounce rates and abandoned carts. 
Outcome: 
- Higher engagement and conversions driven by increased time on site and greater customer confidence.
- Lower support dependency through effective self-service at key decision points.

Improving Patient Engagement Without Adding Risk - Healthcare — Healthcare organizations publish extensive information that patients struggle to interpret. This results in confusion, repeated inquiries, and poor digital experience—without the ability to scale human support. 
Outcome: 
- Improved user experience and trust through clear, engaging digital interactions.
- Scalable, always-on assistance that reduces administrative and support burden without added risk.

Sparse AI:

Intelligent Knowledge Activation - Industry Agnostic — Sparse AI turns static documents into a conversational knowledge layer, enabling natural-language Q&A with source-grounded, structured answers.
Outcome: 
- Faster, trusted knowledge access with cross-document semantic insights and citation-backed answers for compliance and decision-making.
- Lower manual effort with stronger governance through improved auditability, transparency, and institutional trust.

Activating Knowledge from Documents - Industry Agnostic — Sparse AI analyzes archived PDFs to create a semantic knowledge graph that enables natural-language Q&A, pre-built queries, cross-document answers with citations, analytics, and secure role-based access.
Outcome: 
- Faster, accurate access to trusted information with minimal manual review.
- Increased productivity across knowledge-driven workflows.

Sumeru Certificate Lifecycle Management Services

The Impending Future

The Standards Authority Endorsing a 47-Day Certificate Lifetime 
The CA/Browser Forum (the core standards body that defines public TLS/SSL rules) officially passed Ballot SC-081v3 in April 2025 to reduce certificate lifetimes to a 47-day maximum by 2029. 

WHY IT MATTERS:
The CA/B Forum is where certificate authorities (CAs) and browser maintainers like Apple, Google, Microsoft, and Mozilla coordinate web PKI standards. Their vote essentially makes 47-day validity a future requirement on the public internet. 
Major browser vendors, backing the Policy.
The reduction to 47 days was proposed by Apple.

Previously championed in earlier drafts by Google. Voted in favor by all major browser vendors, including:

Maintain trust by discovering, managing, and automating certificates across their entire lifecycle.

1. Certificate & Cryptographic Discovery

TLS Certificates Validity Reducing by 90%

TODAY - 2026:
- Certificate Validity: 398 days ~ 1 renewal / year
- Domain/IP Validity: 398 days ~ 1 renewal / year
- Organization Validity: 825 days ~ 0.4 renewals / year

March 15 - 2026:
- Certificate Validity: 200 days ~ 2 renewals / year
- Domain/IP Validity: 200 days ~ 2 renewals / year
- Organization Validity: 200 days ~ 2 renewals / year

March 15 - 2027:
- Certificate Validity: 100 days ~ 4 renewals / year
- Domain/IP Validity: 100 days ~ 4 renewals / year

March 15 - 2029:
- Certificate Validity: 47 days ~ 9 renewals / year
- Domain/IP Validity: 10 days ~ 39 renewals / year

Source: https://cabforum.org/2025/04/11/ballot-sc081v3-introduce-schedule-of-reducing-validity-and-data-reuse-periods/BR-SC081v3.pdf

Sumeru Quantum Security ( PQC ) Services

Preparing For Post-Quantum Cryptography

US Department of Homeland Security emphasized “Through our partnership with NIST, DHS created a roadmap for those organizations who should be taking action now to prepare for a transition to post-quantum cryptography. This guide will help organizations create effective plans to ensure the continued security of their essential data against the post-quantum threat and prepare for the transition to the new post-quantum cryptography standard when published by NIST.”

Commercial National Security Algorithm Suite 2.0 ( CNSA 2.0 ) formalizes the post-quantum transition approach recommended by DHS

1. Engagement with Standards Organizations: Increase CIO standards engagement
2. Inventory of Critical Data: Identify currently at-risk data
3. Inventory of Cryptographic Technologies: Inventory all cryptographic systems
4. Identification of Internal Standards: Update post-quantum standards
5. Identification of Public Key Cryptography: Identify quantum-vulnerable systems
6. Prioritization of Systems for Replacement: Prioritize systems for transition
7. Plan for Transition: Develop post-quantum transition plans

Timeline:
2021 - 2023: Inventory and prioritize systems
2024: NIST post-quantum cryptography standard published
2024 - 2030: Transition of systems to NIST post-quantum cryptography standard
2030: Cryptographically relevant quantum computer potentially available

The Global Quantum Threat

While classical computers are practically incapable of cracking existing cryptographic algorithms like RSA and ECC, researchers have shown that a cryptographically relevant quantum computer could crack it in a matter of hours.

The National Institute of Standards and Technology Report on Post-Quantum Cryptography found that the first security breaches might come as soon as 2030.

Gartner® predicts that by 2029, quantum computing will be in a position to weaken existing systems to the point that they are considered unsafe to use cryptographically.  
Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin has warned that “The elliptic curves securing Bitcoin and Ethereum “are going to die“. With a 20% probability that quantum computers could break current  cryptography before 2030, the crypto industry has less than four years  to migrate to quantum-resistant systems.”

Sources: https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/quantum-safe-cryptography
https://www.gartner.com/en/articles/post-quantum-cryptography
https://www.cointribune.com/en/vitalik-buterin-sounds-the-alarm-quantum-threat-could-hit-ethereum-before-2028/

What we offer: Sumeru Quantum Security (PQC) Services

1. Post Quantum Risk Assessment & Roadmap: 
Assess quantum exposure across cryptographic assets and data lifecycles
Deliver a prioritized, risk-based PQC remediation plan

2. Cryptographic Discovery & Inventory 
Identify certificates, keys, algorithms, and cryptographic dependencies
Produce an enterprise Cryptographic Bill of Materials (CBOM)

3. Quantum-Safe Architecture & Crypto-Agility Design
Design quantum-safe and hybrid cryptographic reference architectures
Establish crypto-agility principles to support future algorithm changes

4. PQC Integration & Migration 
Define migration approaches from quantum-vulnerable cryptography to PQC
Guide hybrid cryptography adoption across PKI and infrastructure

PKI, Key Management & HSM: Design PQC-capable PKI and certificate lifecycle strategies
Advise on quantum-resistant key management and HSM integration

Operational Readiness, Validation & Rollout Support: Support PQC pilots, validation, and performance assessments
Enable governance, compliance alignment, and enterprise rollout planning

Quantum Computing Will Allow Very Fast Key Breaking

The Bad News:
Asymmetric keys (RSA/ECC) are both vulnerable.
Symmetric keys are vulnerable but much less so.
More than 60% of organizations lack awareness of certificates and keys Across their digital assets

The Good News:
Key breaking takes a lot of qubits, even for modest length RSA keys.
We expect the damage won't be serious until the end of the decade (i.e., in the period between 2028 and 2031).
We have had to change algorithms before.

Source: https://www.appviewx.com/news/new-appviewx-research-reveals-more-than-60-of-organizations-lack-awareness-of-certificates-and-keys-across-their-digital-assets/

Real-time Customer Engagement: Meeting expectations for seamless, personalized interaction across channels.
Innovation Pressure: Keeping ahead with AI-driven solutions and evolving marketing tech.
Cohesive Customer Journeys: Integrating experiences across multiple touchpoints.
Cross-Functional Collaboration: Overcoming organizational silos for unified marketing efforts.
Measuring Marketing ROI: Attribution and effectiveness remain complex challenges.

Top 5 Challenges in  Marketing Technology
AI-Powered Personalization: Driving tailored experiences at scale with agentic AI.
Integrating Martech Stacks: Enabling seamless data flow and unified customer insights.
First-Party Data Strategy: Building privacy-compliant data foundations beyond third-party cookies.
Real-Time Analytics: Unlocking actionable insights for dynamic decision-making.
Privacy and Security: Ensuring data protection while maintaining engagement.

Top 5 Challenges in  Marketing Tech & Analytics
Data Silos and Quality: Disconnected systems hinder full customer visibility and accurate insights.
Attribution Complexity: Multi-channel data complicates performance measurement.
Skill Gaps: Bridging marketing, tech, and analytics expertise in teams.
AI & Predictive Analytics Adoption: Balancing innovation with technical and ethical considerations.
Governance & Privacy Compliance: Managing data responsibly amid evolving regulations.

column6:
Engagement Lifecycle

STAFFING
Staffing process begins by understanding your business goals, the type of role you need filled, the mandate requirements of the candidate and what you expect from the candidate who will fil the open role.

SOURCING
After we establish the required roles, and level of urgency, our dedicated team of expert recruiters would optimize job descriptions and define the interview process.

SELECTION
We submit early profiles for you to review and then calibrate our search parameters before reaching out to tens of thousands of candidates. tI is done to make sure we choose the most suitable candidate to fil the vacant job position.

SCREENING
Our recruitment team would start to
receive qualified applicants several times per day &accordingly would select the candidate's basis tests, interviews, etc.

STATUS
We would keep providing constant communication
with qualified candidates, especially those you would be most interested in filling the position.

SUBMISSION
Upon selection by your team, we would perform necessary background checks and finalize compensation terms and contractual procedures.
